selectedCandidates.title: Evidence-gated research factory for Develop an evidence-chain format for autonomous research agents that binds sources, claims, prototypes, tests, and release artifacts into a replayable public record.
selectedCandidates.technicalField: Autonomous open-source research systems, evidence scoring, and controlled publication workflows.
selectedCandidates.problem: Autonomous research systems can generate plausible artifacts without enough evidence, tests, or publication safety controls.
selectedCandidates.proposedSolution: Use a deterministic factory that plans research questions, discovers and reads public sources, builds a feature matrix, maps candidate novelty gaps, selects invention candidates, generates Open Invention missions, and gates publication on evidence strength.
differentiators: Evidence-bound source discovery and source reading
selectedCandidates.expectedPrototype: A deterministic scorer that ranks factory candidates and blocks weak evidence.
selectedCandidates.expectedTests: Tests for evidence hash binding, scoring caps, and curated release packaging.

topCounterEvidence.overlapDescription: Examples and tests can inform prototype expectations for research agents.
topCounterEvidence.whyItWeakensNovelty: Source-supported overlap means the selected candidate must not present this feature as new without a clearer differentiator.
topCounterEvidence.whyItMayNotFullyCoverCandidate: candidate novelty axis: compare this source claim against Sovryn's replayable evidence gate.
topCounterEvidence.requiredFollowUpSearch: Review the concrete source and search for additional implementations before public release.
topCounterEvidence.recommendedAction: Keep this as counter-evidence in the defensive publication dossier and use careful non-legal language.

invalidationConditions: A concrete source shows the same source-card binding, replay, prototype execution, and curated publication gate combination.
Prototype tests fail to distinguish weak evidence from ready evidence.
strengtheningExperiments: Run additional source reads and verify counter-evidence does not fully cover the candidate.

rejectedCandidates.title: Curated public evidence curator
rejectedCandidates.reason: Rejected because evidence strength is weaker than the selected candidate.

rejectedCandidates.title: Source matrix novelty-gap scorer
rejectedCandidates.reason: Rejected because the selected candidate has stronger combined reproducibility, evidence, and defensive-publication value.

summary.selectionReason: Selected candidates are scored from source evidence strength, source diversity, novelty risk, safety risk, prototype feasibility, testability, defensive-publication value, and reproducibility.
